label: Differentiable memory read/write

node.label: Differentiable memory read/write
node.definition: Content- or address-based operations store and retrieve structured state inside a differentiable model.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Differentiable memory read/write is modeled as depending on Recurrence & feedback state, Embeddings & distributed representations and feeding downstream nodes such as LSTMs & GRUs, xLSTM & linear-recurrent hybrids, Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models.
examples: atlas downstream link to LSTMs & GRUs
atlas downstream link to xLSTM & linear-recurrent hybrids
atlas downstream link to Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Recurrence & feedback state, Embeddings & distributed representations.

dependencies.rationale: Differentiable memory read/write is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Recurrence & feedback state.

In this atlas graph, Differentiable memory read/write has a `uses` dependency on Embeddings & distributed representations because Embeddings & distributed representations is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Differentiable memory read/write has a `uses` dependency on Algorithms & data structures because Algorithms & data structures is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
